titleStmt.title: SII 25.116: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 116. (A.R. No. 116 of 1908.) TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.

summary.p: North wall of the kitchen in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.
This epigraph may be assigned to c. 13th Century on palaeographical grounds.

It records an order (ōlai) issued by the mūlaparushan-mahāsabhai exempting the maḍappuṟam lands of the maṭha called Tiruttuṇḍaittugaiyaṉtirumaḍam from taxes (i.e., as iṟaiyili) like antarāyam, etc and for the utilisation of this income as sabhāviniōgam for the reconstruction of the same maṭha. It is said that the maṭha raised by Uttamaṉ, grandson of Paḷḷikoṇḍāṉ Tirumuṇḍamaḻagiyār of Neḍuvāyil in Aḷagāpuram alias Sōḻiya-Nārāyaṇapuram was caused to be reconstructed by the latter. Also it is mentioned that the mahāsabhai purchased the timber and tiles removed from the maṭha during its stone construction for the price of 20 paḻankāśu fixed for the materials for the purpose of repairing the hall called Dayāpañjaraṉ. A certain Neḍuvāyiluḍaiyāṉ Paḷḷikoṇḍāṉ. Tirumuṇḍamaḻagiyāṉ of Aḷagāpuram alias Śōḻiya-Nārāyaṇapuram is referred to have arranged for the removal of the wood in the above maṭha situated in the southern wing of the southern street in the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam of the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār of Tirupputtūr. For the maṭha referred to in this inscription, a merchant named Tiruchchiṟṟambalamuḍaiyāṉ-uttamaṉ of Neḍuvāyil is stated to have endowed some lands as maḍappuṟam in the 13th year record of Kulaśēkahara. These lands given as maḍappuṟam in the record of Kulaśēkhara seem to have been declared as iṟaiyili once again in the 17th year of the reign of Vīra Pāṇḍya. This gives us a clue to suggest that the taxes seem to have been collected before the 17th year of the reign of Vīra Pāṇḍya, for the reasons not known. The sequence thus quoted helps us to fix this record latter to that of Kulaśēkhara. From the palaeographical grounds and the mention of old maḍappuṟam lands in the present inscription, the king may be identified with Jaṭāvarmaṉ Vīra Pāṇḍya of accession 1281 A.D., whose records are found in Tiruppattūr region. (Vide: A.R.Ep., 1936, para 56).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī mulapaśuṣan mahāsabhaiyom Olai . Aḷakāpuramāṉa ceḻiya nārāyaṇapurattu neṭuvāyi
luṭaiyāṉ paṟṟikoṇṭāṉ tirumuṇṭamaḻakiyān kaṇṭu Uṭaiyārtiruttaḷi yāṇṭa nāyaṉār tirumaṭa
viḷākattu teṟkil tiruvitiyil teṉciṟakil taṅkaḷ peraṉār Uttama ceyattiruttoṇṭattu
kaiyaṉ tirumaṭammāka koppu vāṅka kallāletama muṭaiya dharmmamākac ceykaiyāl Itil vāṅ
kiṉa maraṅkaḷum Ōṭum Ampalan tayāpañcāṉ Aḻivucora Immaraṅkaḷukku vilai niccayitta paḻaṅkā
cu 2 10 Ippaḻaṅkācu Irupatukkum Immaraṅkaḷum Oṭuṅkaik koṇṭu Itukku Immaṭattukku
muṉpu maṭappuṟamāṉa makatam paḷḷattu ciṟucaiy nilamuṟṟu muka� cca tevanila muṟṟum śrī vira
pāṇṭiya tevaṟku 10 7vatu mutal In nilaṅkaḷāl vanta kaṭamai nellum Antarāyam paḻaṅkā
cum tavirntu kuṭuttu sabhāviṉiokamāka Ampalam Aḻivu cora paḻaṅkācu Irupatukkum Immara
ṅkaḷum Oṭuṅkaik koṇṭu Innilaṅkaḷ Ivvāṇṭu mutal paṭṭolai meṟpaṭṭa nilamuṟṟu
m candrādityavaṟ Iṟaiyiliyākap piṭippāṭu paṇṇik kuṭuttom mulavaruṣan mahāsabhai
yom Ippaṭikku Uṭaiyār tiruttaḷiyāṇṭa nāyaṉār śrī koyilile kalveṭṭikkoḷka
Aruḷāl sabhai kaṇakku yaṉ kuḻaiñcāṉāṉa kulacekara muventa veḷān §
Digital edition of SII 25.116 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
